message:
perf(wal_adapter): drop per-read to_vec() on &mut self read_page path

Sibling of `d9c410bb` (`read_page_pinned`). The `&mut self`-taking
`WalBackendAdapter::read_page` fallback — used when the caller does
not hold a pinned snapshot (mixed OLTP, write-path conflict
resolution, adapters that report `supports_pinned_reads() == false`)
— had the exact same wasted allocation shape:

    let mut frame_buf = vec![0u8; self.wal.frame_size()];
    self.wal.read_frame_into(cx, frame_index, &mut frame_buf)?;
    ...
    let data = frame_buf[HEADER..].to_vec(); // alloc #2 + 4 KiB memcpy
    Ok(Some(data))                           // drop frame_buf here

Replace the slice-clone with `copy_within` + `truncate` so the
already-populated `frame_buf` is reused in place: one memmove instead
of an alloc/free/memcpy triple. Return contents (page payload,
`page_size` bytes) and `Vec::len()` are unchanged; `Vec::capacity()`
stays at `frame_size` (page_size + 24), which no caller observes.

Savings per fallback read:
  - 1 × 4096-byte Vec allocation eliminated
  - 1 × 4096-byte Vec free eliminated
  - `.to_vec()` memcpy replaced by in-place `copy_within`

Attribution: `_int_malloc` 3.15% + `cfree` 2.96% self-time at 8t
(tests/artifacts/perf/bd-cnk5d-2t-cliff-verify-20260424/perf-top20.txt).
`read_page_pinned` was one contributor; `read_page` is another on
workloads that do not pin (e.g. writers reading their own staged
frames through this path during commit-phase page assembly).

Verification:
- `cargo check -p fsqlite-core` clean
- `cargo clippy -p fsqlite-core --lib -- -D warnings` clean
- Correctness reduces to the same three-line proof as d9c410bb:
  * frame_buf is length `frame_size = page_size + 24` after
    `read_frame_into`;
  * `copy_within(HEADER.., 0)` moves [24..frame_size] → [0..page_size];
  * `truncate(page_size)` leaves `len = page_size`, identical payload
    bytes to what the old `.to_vec()` produced.

Bead: bd-kve8z

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
